heading: Derivatives clearing organizations
label: 7a–1 Derivatives clearing organizations

children.label: i
children.text: excluded from this chapter by subsection (a)(1)(C)(i), (c), or (f) of ; orsection 2 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a security futures product cleared by a clearing agency registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( et seq.); or15 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: B
children.text: a swap.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to a derivatives clearing organization that is registered with the Commission.

children.heading: Voluntary registration
children.label: b Voluntary registration
children.text: A person that clears 1 or more agreements, contracts, or transactions that are not required to be cleared under this chapter may register with the Commission as a derivatives clearing organization.

children.heading: Registration of derivatives clearing organizations
children.label: c Registration of derivatives clearing organizations

children.heading: Application
children.label: 1 Application
children.text: A person desiring to register as a derivatives clearing organization shall submit to the Commission an application in such form and containing such information as the Commission may require for the purpose of making the determinations required for approval under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Core principles for derivatives clearing organizations
children.label: 2 Core principles for derivatives clearing organizations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To be registered and to maintain registration as a derivatives clearing organization, a derivatives clearing organization shall comply with each core principle described in this paragraph and any requirement that the Commission may impose by rule or regulation pursuant to .section 12a(5) of this title

children.heading: Discretion of derivatives clearing organization
children.label: ii Discretion of derivatives clearing organization
children.text: Subject to any rule or regulation prescribed by the Commission, a derivatives clearing organization shall have reasonable discretion in establishing the manner by which the derivatives clearing organization complies with each core principle described in this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall have adequate financial, operational, and managerial resources, as determined by the Commission, to discharge each responsibility of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.heading: Minimum amount of financial resources
children.label: ii Minimum amount of financial resources
I
children.text: enable the organization to meet its financial obligations to its members and participants notwithstanding a default by the member or participant creating the largest financial exposure for that organization in extreme but plausible market conditions; and

children.label: II
children.text: enable the derivatives clearing organization to cover the operating costs of the derivatives clearing organization for a period of 1 year (as calculated on a rolling basis).

children.label: I
children.text: appropriate admission and continuing eligibility standards (including sufficient financial resources and operational capacity to meet obligations arising from participation in the derivatives clearing organization) for members of, and participants in, the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: appropriate standards for determining the eligibility of agreements, contracts, or transactions submitted to the derivatives clearing organization for clearing.

children.heading: Required procedures
children.label: ii Required procedures
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall establish and implement procedures to verify, on an ongoing basis, the compliance of each participation and membership requirement of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.label: I
children.text: be objective;

children.label: II
children.text: be publicly disclosed; and

children.label: III
children.text: permit fair and open access.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall ensure that the derivatives clearing organization possesses the ability to manage the risks associated with discharging the responsibilities of the derivatives clearing organization through the use of appropriate tools and procedures.

children.label: I
children.text: not less than once during each business day of the derivatives clearing organization, measure the credit exposures of the derivatives clearing organization to each member and participant of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: monitor each exposure described in subclause (I) periodically during the business day of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.heading: Limitation of exposure to potential losses from defaults
children.label: iii Limitation of exposure to potential losses from defaults
I
children.text: the operations of the derivatives clearing organization would not be disrupted; and

children.label: II
children.text: nondefaulting members or participants would not be exposed to losses that nondefaulting members or participants cannot anticipate or control.

children.heading: Margin requirements
children.label: iv Margin requirements
children.text: The margin required from each member and participant of a derivatives clearing organization shall be sufficient to cover potential exposures in normal market conditions.

children.heading: Requirements regarding models and parameters
children.label: v Requirements regarding models and parameters
I
children.text: risk-based; and

children.label: II
children.text: reviewed on a regular basis.

children.label: i
children.text: complete money settlements on a timely basis (but not less frequently than once each business day);

children.label: ii
children.text: employ money settlement arrangements to eliminate or strictly limit the exposure of the derivatives clearing organization to settlement bank risks (including credit and liquidity risks from the use of banks to effect money settlements);

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure that money settlements are final when effected;

children.label: iv
children.text: maintain an accurate record of the flow of funds associated with each money settlement;

children.label: v
children.text: possess the ability to comply with each term and condition of any permitted netting or offset arrangement with any other clearing organization;

children.label: vi
children.text: regarding physical settlements, establish rules that clearly state each obligation of the derivatives clearing organization with respect to physical deliveries; and

children.label: vii
children.text: ensure that each risk arising from an obligation described in clause (vi) is identified and managed.

children.heading: Required standards and procedures
children.label: i Required standards and procedures
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall establish standards and procedures that are designed to protect and ensure the safety of member and participant funds and assets.

children.heading: Holding of funds and assets
children.label: ii Holding of funds and assets
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall hold member and participant funds and assets in a manner by which to minimize the risk of loss or of delay in the access by the derivatives clearing organization to the assets and funds.

children.heading: Permissible investments
children.label: iii Permissible investments
children.text: Funds and assets invested by a derivatives clearing organization shall be held in instruments with minimal credit, market, and liquidity risks.

children.label: I
children.text: become insolvent; or

children.label: II
children.text: otherwise default on the obligations of the members or participants to the derivatives clearing organization.

children.label: I
children.text: clearly state the default procedures of the derivatives clearing organization;

children.label: II
children.text: make publicly available the default rules of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: aa
children.text: to contain losses and liquidity pressures; and

children.label: bb
children.text: to continue meeting each obligation of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.label: I
children.text: the effective monitoring and enforcement of compliance with the rules of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: the resolution of disputes;

children.label: ii
children.text: have the authority and ability to discipline, limit, suspend, or terminate the activities of a member or participant due to a violation by the member or participant of any rule of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: report to the Commission regarding rule enforcement activities and sanctions imposed against members and participants as provided in clause (ii).

children.label: i
children.text: establish and maintain a program of risk analysis and oversight to identify and minimize sources of operational risk through the development of appropriate controls and procedures, and automated systems, that are reliable, secure, and have adequate scalable capacity;

children.label: I
children.text: the timely recovery and resumption of operations of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: the fulfillment of each obligation and responsibility of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: iii
children.text: periodically conduct tests to verify that the backup resources of the derivatives clearing organization are sufficient to ensure daily processing, clearing, and settlement.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: J Reporting
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall provide to the Commission all information that the Commission determines to be necessary to conduct oversight of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.label: i
children.text: in a form and manner that is acceptable to the Commission; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for a period of not less than 5 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall provide to market participants sufficient information to enable the market participants to identify and evaluate accurately the risks and costs associated with using the services of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.heading: Availability of information
children.label: ii Availability of information
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall make information concerning the rules and operating and default procedures governing the clearing and settlement systems of the derivatives clearing organization available to market participants.

children.label: I
children.text: the terms and conditions of each contract, agreement, and transaction cleared and settled by the derivatives clearing organization;

children.label: II
children.text: each clearing and other fee that the derivatives clearing organization charges the members and participants of the derivatives clearing organization;

children.label: III
children.text: the margin-setting methodology, and the size and composition, of the financial resource package of the derivatives clearing organization;

children.label: IV
children.text: daily settlement prices, volume, and open interest for each contract settled or cleared by the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: V
children.text: any other matter relevant to participation in the settlement and clearing activities of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.label: i
children.text: enter into, and abide by the terms of, each appropriate and applicable domestic and international information-sharing agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: use relevant information obtained from each agreement described in clause (i) in carrying out the risk management program of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.label: i
children.text: adopt any rule or take any action that results in any unreasonable restraint of trade; or

children.label: ii
children.text: impose any material anticompetitive burden.

children.label: I
children.text: to fulfill public interest requirements; and

children.label: II
children.text: to permit the consideration of the views of owners and participants.

children.label: I
children.text: directors;

children.label: II
children.text: members of any disciplinary committee;

children.label: III
children.text: members of the derivatives clearing organization;

children.label: IV
children.text: any other individual or entity with direct access to the settlement or clearing activities of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: V
children.text: any party affiliated with any individual or entity described in this clause.

children.label: i
children.text: establish and enforce rules to minimize conflicts of interest in the decision-making process of the derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establish a process for resolving conflicts of interest described in clause (i).

children.heading: Composition of governing boards
children.label: Q Composition of governing boards
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall ensure that the composition of the governing board or committee of the derivatives clearing organization includes market participants.

children.heading: Legal risk
children.label: R Legal risk
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall have a well-founded, transparent, and enforceable legal framework for each aspect of the activities of the derivatives clearing organization.

children.heading: Orders concerning competition
children.label: 3 Orders concerning competition
children.text: A derivatives clearing organization may request the Commission to issue an order concerning whether a rule or practice of the applicant is the least anticompetitive means of achieving the objectives, purposes, and policies of this chapter.

children.heading: Existing derivatives clearing organizations
children.label: d Existing derivatives clearing organizations
children.text: A derivatives clearing organization shall be deemed to be registered under this section to the extent that the derivatives clearing organization clears agreements, contracts, or transactions for a board of trade that has been designated by the Commission as a contract market for such agreements, contracts, or transactions before .December 21, 2000

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If a proceeding under results in the suspension or revocation of the registration of a derivatives clearing organization, or if a derivatives clearing organization withdraws from registration, the Commission, on notice to the derivatives clearing organization, may apply to the appropriate United States district court where the derivatives clearing organization is located for the appointment of a trustee.section 7b of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the court may take exclusive jurisdiction over the derivatives clearing organization and the records and assets of the derivatives clearing organization, wherever located; and

children.label: B
children.text: if the court takes jurisdiction under subparagraph (A), the court shall appoint the Commission, or a person designated by the Commission, as trustee with power to take possession and continue to operate or terminate the operations of the derivatives clearing organization in an orderly manner for the protection of participants, subject to such terms and conditions as the court may prescribe.

children.heading: Linking of regulated clearing facilities
children.label: f Linking of regulated clearing facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall facilitate the linking or coordination of derivatives clearing organizations registered under this chapter with other regulated clearance facilities for the coordinated settlement of cleared transactions. In order to minimize systemic risk, under no circumstances shall a derivatives clearing organization be compelled to accept the counterparty credit risk of another clearing organization.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 2 Coordination
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Commission shall coordinate with the Federal banking agencies and the Securities and Exchange Commission.

children.heading: Existing depository institutions and clearing agencies
children.label: g Existing depository institutions and clearing agencies
A
children.text: the depository institution cleared swaps as a multilateral clearing organization; or

children.label: B
children.text: the clearing agency cleared swaps.

children.heading: Conversion of depository institutions
children.label: 2 Conversion of depository institutions
children.text: A depository institution to which this subsection applies may, by the vote of the shareholders owning not less than 51 percent of the voting interests of the depository institution, be converted into a State corporation, partnership, limited liability company, or similar legal form pursuant to a plan of conversion, if the conversion is not in contravention of applicable State law.

children.heading: Sharing of information
children.label: 3 Sharing of information
children.text: The Securities and Exchange Commission shall make available to the Commission, upon request, all information determined to be relevant by the Securities and Exchange Commission regarding a clearing agency deemed to be registered with the Commission under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Exemptions
children.label: h Exemptions
children.text: The Commission may exempt, conditionally or unconditionally, a derivatives clearing organization from registration under this section for the clearing of swaps if the Commission determines that the derivatives clearing organization is subject to comparable, comprehensive supervision and regulation by the Securities and Exchange Commission or the appropriate government authorities in the home country of the organization. Such conditions may include, but are not limited to, requiring that the derivatives clearing organization be available for inspection by the Commission and make available all information requested by the Commission.

children.heading: Designation of chief compliance officer
children.label: i Designation of chief compliance officer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization shall designate an individual to serve as a chief compliance officer.

children.label: A
children.text: report directly to the board or to the senior officer of the derivatives clearing organization;

children.label: B
children.text: review the compliance of the derivatives clearing organization with respect to the core principles described in subsection (c)(2);

children.label: C
children.text: in consultation with the board of the derivatives clearing organization, a body performing a function similar to the board of the derivatives clearing organization, or the senior officer of the derivatives clearing organization, resolve any conflicts of interest that may arise;

children.label: D
children.text: be responsible for administering each policy and procedure that is required to be established pursuant to this section;

children.label: E
children.text: ensure compliance with this chapter (including regulations) relating to agreements, contracts, or transactions, including each rule prescribed by the Commission under this section;

children.label: i
children.text: compliance office review;

children.label: ii
children.text: look-back;

children.label: iii
children.text: internal or external audit finding;

children.label: iv
children.text: self-reported error; or

children.label: v
children.text: validated complaint; and

children.label: G
children.text: establish and follow appropriate procedures for the handling, management response, remediation, retesting, and closing of noncompliance issues.

children.label: i
children.text: the compliance of the derivatives clearing organization of the compliance officer with respect to this chapter (including regulations); and

children.label: ii
children.text: each policy and procedure of the derivatives clearing organization of the compliance officer (including the code of ethics and conflict of interest policies of the derivatives clearing organization).

children.label: i
children.text: accompany each appropriate financial report of the derivatives clearing organization that is required to be furnished to the Commission pursuant to this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include a certification that, under penalty of law, the compliance report is accurate and complete.

children.heading: Reporting requirements1So in original. No subsec. (j) has been enacted.1
children.label: k Reporting requirements1So in original. No subsec. (j) has been enacted.1

children.heading: Duty of derivatives clearing organizations
children.label: 1 Duty of derivatives clearing organizations
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization that clears swaps shall provide to the Commission all information that is determined by the Commission to be necessary to perform each responsibility of the Commission under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: swaps data reported to swap data repositories; and

children.label: B
children.text: swaps traded on swap execution facilities.

children.heading: Reports on security-based swap agreements to be shared with the Securities and Exchange Commission
children.label: 3 Reports on security-based swap agreements to be shared with the Securities and Exchange Commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A derivatives clearing organization that clears security-based swap agreements (as defined in ) shall, upon request, open to inspection and examination to the Securities and Exchange Commission all books and records relating to such security-based swap agreements, consistent with the confidentiality and disclosure requirements of .section 1a(47)(A)(v) of this titlesection 12 of this title

children.heading: Jurisdiction
children.label: B Jurisdiction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall affect the exclusive jurisdiction of the Commission to prescribe recordkeeping and reporting requirements for a derivatives clearing organization that is registered with the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: the Board;

children.label: B
children.text: the Securities and Exchange Commission;

children.label: C
children.text: each appropriate prudential regulator;

children.label: D
children.text: the Financial Stability Oversight Council;

children.label: E
children.text: the Department of Justice; and

children.label: i
children.text: foreign financial supervisors (including foreign futures authorities);

children.label: ii
children.text: foreign central banks; and

children.label: iii
children.text: foreign ministries.

children.heading: Confidentiality agreement
children.label: 5 Confidentiality agreement
children.text: Before the Commission may share information with any entity described in paragraph (4), the Commission shall receive a written agreement from each entity stating that the entity shall abide by the confidentiality requirements described in relating to the information on swap transactions that is provided.section 12 of this title

children.heading: Public information
children.label: 6 Public information
children.text: Each derivatives clearing organization that clears swaps shall provide to the Commission (including any designee of the Commission) information under paragraph (2) in such form and at such frequency as is required by the Commission to comply with the public reporting requirements contained in .section 2(a)(13) of this title
